> Thanks for the Redfish suite — overall design is solid (session-scoped mTLS fixture, both off→on transitions checked for `PowerCycle`, negative cert tests). A few blocking issues in `tests/redfish/conftest.py` that I'd like resolved before merge.
> 
> ### Blocking
> **1. Password leaks to `ps` and shell-injection risk in `_bmc_ssh` / `_bmc_scp` (`conftest.py:108-129`)**
> 
> ```python
> ssh_cmd = "sshpass -p {pass_} ssh ... {cmd}".format(pass_=bmc_pass, ..., cmd=cmd)
> subprocess.run(ssh_cmd, shell=True, check=True, ...)
> ```
> 
> `sshpass -p <pw>` puts the cleartext BMC password in the process argv (visible to any `ps` on the sonic-mgmt host) for the lifetime of every SSH/SCP call. `bmc_pass` is also interpolated unquoted into a `shell=True` string — works today only because `creds.yml` happens to be shell-safe; will break or execute injected shell the moment the password contains `$`, `` ` ``, `'`, `"`, space, `;`, etc. `_bmc_scp` has the same pattern.
> 
> Suggested fix: use `sshpass -e` with `env={"SSHPASS": bmc_pass}`, drop `shell=True`, pass argv as a list.
> 
> **2. `_bmc_ssh` contract: docstring promises `(stdout, stderr, returncode)` but `check=True` raises (`conftest.py:106-123`)**
> 
> ```python
> def _bmc_ssh(...):
>     """... Returns (stdout, stderr, returncode)."""
>     result = subprocess.run(ssh_cmd, shell=True, check=True, ...)
> ```
> 
> With `check=True`, any non-zero exit raises `CalledProcessError` instead of returning the documented `returncode`. The only call site that wraps this in `try/except` is `_bmcweb_running`; every other caller (including `bmc_exec` used by tests) will surface an opaque traceback instead of a clean assertion. Either drop `check=True` and let callers inspect `rc`, or update the docstring + all call sites to expect raising.
> 
> **3. Inconsistent quoting between `bmc_exec` and direct `_bmc_ssh` callers (`conftest.py:82` and e.g. `:152`)**
> 
> ```python
> # bmc_exec wraps:
> def _exec(cmd):
>     return _bmc_ssh(..., "'{}'".format(cmd))
> 
> # _bmcweb_running also wraps:
> _bmc_ssh(..., "'docker exec {} supervisorctl status bmcweb'".format(BMCWEB_CONTAINER))
> ```
> 
> `_bmc_ssh` interpolates `cmd` raw into the ssh command line, so the implicit convention is "caller must pre-quote." That convention isn't enforced, isn't documented, and breaks the first time any caller does `bmc_exec("docker exec redfish bash -c 'rm /tmp/x'")` — `bmc_exec`'s outer `'...'` wrapper collides with the embedded `'` and the remote shell mis-parses. Please consolidate: have `_bmc_ssh` use argv-list `subprocess.run([..., "ssh", ..., cmd])` with `shell=False` so callers never quote.
> 
> **4. `bmc_creds` reads a hardcoded relative path and can silently return `password=None` (`conftest.py:34-49`)**
> 
> ```python
> creds_file = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__),
>                           "../../ansible/group_vars/all/creds.yml")
> ...
> return {"user": creds_data.get("sonic_login"),
>         "password": default_passwords[0] if default_passwords else None}
> ```
> 
> Three concerns: (a) the relative path only works in a sonic-mgmt checkout with that exact layout — containerized or downstream consumers will hit `FileNotFoundError` at fixture collection time; (b) if `sonic_default_passwords` is empty, `password=None` flows into `sshpass -p None ...` and surfaces as a confusing auth failure deep in a test, not a clear setup error; (c) using SONiC switch credentials (`sonic_login` / `sonic_default_passwords`) for the BMC assumes they match — most BMC infra has dedicated BMC creds. Please switch to `pytest_addoption` (`--bmc-username` / `--bmc-password`) or BMC-specific inventory vars, and `pytest_require(creds["password"], "BMC password not configured")` to fail loudly.
> 
> ### Please clarify
> **`bmc_ip` fixture: docstring says `<dut>-bmc`, code reads `duts[0]` (`conftest.py:25-31`)**
> 
> ```python
> """Return the BMC mgmt IP from the <dut>-bmc inventory entry."""
> dut_name = tbinfo["duts"][0]
> return duthosts[dut_name].host.options["inventory_manager"]
>     .get_host(dut_name).get_vars()["ansible_host"]
> ```
> 
> The code never appends `-bmc` — it just returns `ansible_host` of `tbinfo["duts"][0]`. In the BMC testbed entries I'm familiar with, `duts[0]` is the SONiC switch and the BMC is a separate `<switch>-bmc` inventory host, so this would aim Redfish/SSH at the switch mgmt IP. Could you confirm which is intended for `bmc-dual-mgmt` / `bmc-shared-mgmt`? If the docstring is right, the code likely needs `dut_name + "-bmc"`.
> 
> Non-blocking nits (banner-strip hardcoded to `Debian GNU/Linux 13`, `EXPECTED_MEMBERS={"bmc","bios","switch"}` blocks adding platforms, `test_reset_invalid_type` only checks `"error" in body` instead of asserting a `RedfishError` shape, no finalizer fixture so a failing `test_reset_power_cycle` leaves the x86 CPU in reset) — happy to file separately if useful, but the four above are the merge-blockers.
> 
> Reviewed by AI agent on behalf of Ying.

Thanks @yxieca for the review. Following the BMC HLD (#22688), which models the BMC as a DUT (`SonicHost`) and uses `duthost.shell()` in its examples, I moved the SSH/credential layer onto the `SonicHost` framework — `duthost.shell()` for commands and `duthost.copy()` for file transfer (the standard framework equivalent of `scp`) — removing `_bmc_ssh`, `_bmc_scp`, and `bmc_creds` entirely. That resolves the four blocking items at the root:

- **1. Password in `ps` / injection** — no `sshpass`, no `shell=True`; auth is over Ansible with inventory creds, so the password never hits a command line.
- **2. `check=True` vs. contract** — `duthost.shell(..., module_ignore_errors=True)` returns `rc` without raising, so `bmc_exec` honestly returns `(stdout, stderr, rc)`; setup steps fail loudly, teardown is best-effort.
- **3. Inconsistent quoting** — commands go to the remote shell as a single arg, so no caller pre-quotes (the `'{}'` wrapper is gone).
- **4. `bmc_creds`** — deleted; the BMC authenticates from its own inventory entry (`ansible_user`/`ansible_ssh_pass`), so no relative path, no silent `None` (`pyrequire(is_bmc())` gates non-BMC DUTs), and BMC-specific creds. This is the inventory-vars option you suggested.

**`bmc_ip` clarification:** in these topologies the `dut` entry *is* the `<switch>-bmc` host (the host-side switch is the separate `bmc_host`), so `duts[0]` is the BMC and the code was correct — I reworded the docstring and switched to `duthost.mgmt_ip`.

**Nits:** banner strip is gone with `_bmc_ssh`; `test_reset_invalid_type` now asserts the `RedfishError` shape (`code`+`message`), which also fixed the CodeQL `error_body` finding; added an autouse finalizer that restores the CPU to `On` after each reset test. On `EXPECTED_MEMBERS` — it's a required-*subset* check (`EXPECTED_MEMBERS - member_ids`), so it doesn't block adding platforms.

> Critical: Test itself fails — lossy queues show frame loss and NAKs The PR's own output shows 2/7 checks failed:
> 
> "No loss" — flows on DSCP 0 and 1 (lossy queues) show frame_delta of ~17,000 "No NAK/sequence error" — same lossy flows have significant NAKs and sequence errors
> 
> This is a fundamental test logic vs. actual behavior mismatch: in a "no congestion" scenario, lossy queues still see retransmissions ? Do you know why ? Could be due to hash collisions on shared egress ports. The checks apply loss/NAK assertions across all flows including lossy, but lossy queues don't have PFC protection, so some drops are expected
> 
> Do you think the "no loss" and "no NAK" checks in build_common_checks() should filter to only lossless DSCP values (3, 4), OR use a tolerance for lossy queues ? Please check. Also, Can you please make this test pass with all checks ?



In these tests we cannot use traffic with lossy priorities. As this is stateful traffic, if we include both lossless and lossy priorities in the flows, once we oversubscribe the Rx port, it will drop few lossy packets and it will keep sending Acks and after certain retries, it stops sending packets for those lossy priorities. We cannot tell which lossy flow it will stop. Hence for RoCEv2 we have use only lossless priorities.

Review note (non-blocking):
- **Route aggregation behavior:** Changing overwrite to append may introduce duplicates or unintended semantics for topologies other than BGP_SCALE_T1S. Confirm scope or add deduplication/clear rationale.
- **RX quiescence wait must be bounded:** Ensure `wait_for_rx_quiescence()` has a hard max timeout and cannot hang indefinitely under continuous traffic; document or expose thresholds.
- **Timeout handling visibility:** Nanomsg timeout handling must not silently mask failures—ensure logging + retry/threshold/clear failure signal so tests don’t pass without traffic.
- Add comments/docstrings explaining quiescence algorithm and ECMP/shared-route rationale.
- Extract poll interval/stability thresholds as constants.
- Log observed drain duration and/or delta counters for debugging.
- Avoid over-broad exception handling or ensure appropriate severity.

Reviewed by AI agent on behalf of Ying.

@yxieca
 Thanks for the review. Addressing each point:

  1. Route append duplicates  — The append is safe for all topologies. The BGP_SCALE_T1S loop only runs when  "topo_name in BGP_SCALE_T1S" . For all other topologies, topo_routes[k] is initialized as {}, so `.get(IPV4, [])` returns [] and the append is equivalent to =. No duplicate risk.

  2. Max timeout on wait_for_rx_quiescence — covered by yejianquan — will add a 'max_timeout=120'  parameter. If counters don't stabilize within 120s, the loop breaks with a warning log and proceeds with current counters.

  3. Silently mask failures — Failures are not silently masked. '_send_with_retry' returns 'False'  on exhaustion, the caller tracks it in 'retry_failures', and logs the total at thread exit: "%d packets are sent (%d retry failures)".

  4. Add docstrings — 'wait_for_rx_quiescence' already has a docstring explaining the nn_agent pipeline drain and why polling replaces fixed sleep. The ECMP/route-append rationale is in the commit message.

  5. Extract constants — These are already named function default parameters (`stable_secs=10`, `poll_interval=1`), which serve the same purpose. Extracting to module-level constants would add indirection without improving readability.

  6. Log drain duration — 'calculate_downtime' already logs all rx/tx counters, totals, missing packet count, pps, and duration. Adding more logging in the quiescence function would be redundant.

  7. Over-broad exception handling  — The 'except Exception' with re-raise is intentional. ptf_dataplane.send() raises nnpy.NNError on timeout, but test_ipv6_bgp_scale.py doesn't import nnpy ( only available on the PTF host?). The guard if "timed out" not in str(e).lower(): raise ensures non-timeout exceptions propagate immediately. The only other caller
 of dataplane.send() in sonic-mgmt (test_xon_xoff.py) uses no error handling at all — we added retry handling specifically because the scale test (512 ports, 1024 exaBGP processes) triggers nn_agent CPU starvation that smaller tests never hit.

Thank you @deepak-singhal0408 for the incredibly thorough review. The depth of analysis taught me a lot. I genuinely appreciate the time and effort that went into this.

Apologies for the delay; I wanted to ensure the changes were solid. Since I significantly rewrote the test, I split the updates into logical commits rather than one large blob. Hope this structure clarifies my reasoning. To make re-review easier, here's how each feedback maps to the commits:

| # | Feedback | Solution | Commit |
|---|----------|----------|--------|
| 1 | `L192`: Skip condition wrong — `frr_mgmt_framework_config` doesn't control mgmtd | Now checks `pgrep -x mgmtd` in bgp containers | https://github.com/sonic-net/sonic-mgmt/pull/21429/commits/8b8a44ed3663529d1c2feccaa6deb1bd8125d5aa |
| 2 | `L16`: T2 topology missing | Added T2 to marker + `dut_with_default_route` fixture | https://github.com/sonic-net/sonic-mgmt/pull/21429/commits/8b8a44ed3663529d1c2feccaa6deb1bd8125d5aa |
| 3 | `L197`: No race amplification | Added vtysh race loop spawning competing sessions during `config_reload` | https://github.com/sonic-net/sonic-mgmt/pull/21429/commits/370217b0690b0f25f696d85d15186eddf77a5425 |
| 4 | `L170`: Static routes don't trigger race | Replaced with FRR prefix-list bloat via `vtysh -f` | https://github.com/sonic-net/sonic-mgmt/pull/21429/commits/9fe99fb3dd4784ec3da01dd59698485f16b50557 |
| 5 | `L108`: Mixed implicit/explicit returns | `_get_remote_ip` removed entirely (CodeQL resolved by deletion) | https://github.com/sonic-net/sonic-mgmt/pull/21429/commits/9fe99fb3dd4784ec3da01dd59698485f16b50557 |
| 6 | `L1`: Filename misleading | Renamed to `test_frr_set_src_route_map.py` | https://github.com/sonic-net/sonic-mgmt/pull/21429/commits/4aea95d6da30c28b34f536c21920d3c708ceca17 |
| 7 | `L157`: Per-prefix cleanup loop slow | Single `no route-map` + `no prefix-list` command | https://github.com/sonic-net/sonic-mgmt/pull/21429/commits/9fe99fb3dd4784ec3da01dd59698485f16b50557 |

Note feedback 1,2 are in one commit; 4,5,7 are also grouped together (same code area); 3 and 6 are standalone.

I have resolved the conversations that are addressed. I've also updated the PR description to reference the original issue and upstream fix.

Let me know if there is anything else that needs to be fixed, thanks!
